title: Impulse — 2D Physics Engine
description: A 2D rigid-body physics engine built from scratch in TypeScript — GJK/EPA collision, a unified radius-aware narrowphase for circles, capsules and rounded polygons, and conservative-advancement continuous collision (CCD) so fast bodies never tunnel. The solver is a warm-started sequential-impulse method with split-impulse position correction AND an exact two-point block LCP (Box2D-Lite's four-case analysis) so wide stacks settle flat. It carries a from-scratch buoyancy & fluid-drag subsystem (real Archimedes lift ρ_fluid·A_submerged·(−g) at the centre of buoyancy, submerged area integrated in closed form against the waterline) and spatial queries (AABB region queries + a conservative-advancement convex shape-cast). New in v4 — a machine-builder's toolkit: the Box2D joint family is now complete with a pulley (length-ratio over two ground anchors), a gear joint (couples two revolute/prismatic joints by a ratio — gear trains & rack-and-pinion) and a motor joint (an overpowerable actuator that stalls under load); conveyor surfaces (a per-body tangentSpeed folded into the friction constraint so belts drag bodies up to speed); a radial-impulse / explosion field with distance falloff and static-geometry ray occlusion; and breakable joints that measure their own reaction load and snap past a force/torque budget. New in v5 — a second physics paradigm: from-scratch **soft bodies** via XPBD (extended position-based dynamics). Particles held by compliant distance, bending and area-preservation constraints, advanced with the step-size-independent 'small-steps' substep scheme, collide with every rigid shape through the engine's own core+radius math and exchange momentum two-ways — so a jelly shoves a crate and a falling crate dents a cloth hammock. Builders for pressurised blobs, pinnable cloth/membranes, ropes and lattice solids, a smooth jelly/cloth/rope renderer, and click-to-grab dragging. NEW in v6 — real-time **rigid-body fracture**: a brittle body shatters into convex **Voronoi shards** built by clipping its hull against one perpendicular-bisector half-plane per seed (a from-scratch power-free Voronoi, glass-style rings of seeds focused on the impact point). The split is a true rigid decomposition — every shard inherits the parent's velocity field vᵢ = v + ω×(cᵢ−C), so the shards' masses, **linear momentum** and **angular momentum about the parent's centre of mass** all sum back to the parent's exactly (the suite asserts Σm, Σp and ΣL = I·ω to 1e-9) and the object keeps moving as one until the contact solver prises the pieces apart. The world auto-shatters any brittle body whose strongest contact this step beats its toughness, with a generation cap that bounds the cascade; a click-to-shatter pointer tool and impact-spark renderer round it out. NEW in v7 — a third physics paradigm: an incompressible **Position-Based Fluid** (SPH, Macklin & Müller 2013) built from scratch — 2-D poly6/spiky kernels, a uniform-grid spatial hash for O(n) neighbour finding, the density-constraint Jacobi solve with CFM relaxation, XSPH viscosity, vorticity confinement and jet emitters, all **two-way coupled** to the rigid world through the same particle-vs-shape collision the soft engine uses (and a two-way position correction that gives genuine hydrostatic buoyancy — a light raft floats, a dense ingot sinks). A speed-shaded metaball renderer and a 💧 spray tool that paints water into any scene. NEW in v8 — a FOURTH physics paradigm and the engine's first true continuum solver: **finite-element elasticity**. Deformable solids are meshed into constant-strain triangles and stepped with **co-rotational linear FEM** under an **implicit (backward-Euler) integrator** — each frame factors out every element's rigid rotation by a closed-form 2-D polar decomposition (so large rotations cost zero spurious energy, the failure mode that wrecks plain linear FEM) and solves one symmetric-positive-definite system M+hC+h²K′ with a matrix-free, Jacobi-preconditioned **conjugate-gradient** solver that never assembles the global stiffness. Materials carry real **Young's modulus / Poisson ratio / Rayleigh damping**; pinned nodes become Dirichlet boundaries via a projected CG; the von-Mises stress field is recovered and drawn as a live heatmap that lights up the load path; and FEM nodes collide and exchange momentum with the rigid world through the same core+radius bridge the soft and fluid solvers use (grab and stretch a node with the pointer). The headline proof: a clamped cantilever sagging under its own weight **converges to the Euler–Bernoulli tip deflection δ = ρ·H·g·L⁴/(8·E·I)** as the mesh refines (0.52 → 0.79 → 0.94 → 0.97 of theory), doubling E exactly halves the deflection, a large rigid rotation stores zero strain energy, and a uniaxial strain reproduces the analytic plane-stress von-Mises stress. NEW in v9 — a FIFTH physics paradigm and a second continuum solver: the **Material Point Method (MLS-MPM)**, the hybrid Eulerian–Lagrangian method (Hu et al. 2018) that carries all state on a cloud of particles but computes forces on a transient background grid it throws away each step — so one solver handles materials that genuinely *flow, split and merge*, which a fixed FEM mesh cannot. Four constitutive models share a single quadratic-B-spline transfer: **fixed-corotated** elastic jelly, **Stomakhin (2013) snow** (singular-value-clamped elastoplasticity that packs and crumbles), **Drucker–Prager sand** (Klár et al. 2016 — a Hencky-strain return-mapping onto a friction cone, so a poured column collapses to a real **angle of repose** and carries no tension), and a weakly-compressible **MPM water**. The affine **APIC** transfer (Jiang et al. 2015) is **angular-momentum-conserving by construction** — the verifier confirms the grid's angular momentum equals the particles' APIC angular momentum (orbital + affine spin) to 1e-14 — and the whole system is **two-way coupled** to the rigid world through the very same particle↔shape bridge the SPH and soft solvers use, so a heavy boulder punches a crater into a sand bed and the ejecta rains back. A from-scratch closed-form 2×2 SVD (reconstructs to 1e-15) drives every constitutive model, a 🏖 sand / ❄ snow / 🟢 jelly / 💧 paint tool pours material into any scene, and ten new scenes (Sand Pile, Hourglass, Snow Splat, Jelly Blocks, Impact Crater, Sand Dam Break, Material Garden, Sinking Crates, Sand Seesaw, MPM Sandbox) join the catalogue. Nine joint types, a dynamic AABB-tree broadphase, islands & sleeping, and a 60-scene interactive playground (Sand Pile, Hourglass, Impact Crater, Snow Splat, Sand Seesaw, FEM Cantilever, FEM Load Bridge, FEM Jelly, Dam Break, Fountain, Communicating Vessels, Waterwheel, Glass Pane, Shatter Tower, …) backed by a live 202-check correctness suite that re-derives every claim from an analytic reference (the cantilever converges to beam theory; co-rotational rotation invariance to 1e-6; FEM momentum conserved and bit-for-bit deterministic; the SPH poly6 kernel integrates to 1; a settling fluid column stays incompressible at ~1.0 ρ₀ and conserves mass; Voronoi cells tile their parent exactly; fracture conserves mass, area & momentum; soft-body momentum conserved to ~1e-11; the closed-form 2×2 SVD reconstructs every matrix to 1e-15; the MLS-MPM transfer conserves linear momentum and APIC conserves angular momentum to 1e-14; the corotated stress vanishes under a pure rotation; Drucker–Prager sand settles at a finite angle of repose; Stomakhin snow compacts plastically; MPM↔rigid coupling conserves total momentum to 1e-14; fully deterministic).
